questions.question: Explain why multilayer perceptrons can solve nonlinear problems while single-layer perceptrons cannot.
expectedPoints: Single-layer perceptrons create linear decision boundaries
Multiple layers with nonlinear activation functions enable learning complex patterns
Hidden layers allow the network to learn hierarchical features
Example demonstrating XOR problem limitation of single-layer networks

questions.question: Compare and contrast Convolutional Neural Networks and Recurrent Neural Networks, including their architectures, applications, and appropriate use cases.
expectedPoints: CNN architecture: convolutional layers, pooling layers, feature detection
RNN architecture: recurrent connections, memory cells, sequential processing
CNN applications: image processing, computer vision tasks
RNN applications: sequence data, language processing, time series
Use case examples for each network type
Performance considerations for different data types
